meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-friendly guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' barrier ends by shipping a functional, AI-powered application. By leveraging the Antigravity project template and Claude Code, you bypass boilerplate fatigue. Focus on core logic, deploy a live URL, and demonstrate technical competence that hiring managers can verify instantly. Stop studying, start building.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio page is the silent killer of junior careers. You have the theory, the tutorials, and the ambition, yet you lack the one thing that matters in a technical interview: a live, production-grade application. The industry doesn't reward potential; it rewards proof of work.

We are changing the narrative today. By integrating the Antigravity project template with Claude Code, you are moving from a student to an AI builder. This isn't just a coding exercise; it is a battle-tested workflow designed to turn your initial concept into a deployed asset that forces recruiters to take notice.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Establish the project structure using the Antigravity template to ensure industry-standard architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity base template with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase integration.

steps.title: Define the Core AI Logic
steps.description: Inject intelligence into your app by configuring the API layer to interact with LLMs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an AI service layer that handles streaming responses from an LLM, including error handling and context management.

steps.title: Build the User Interface
steps.description: Use component-driven development to create a responsive, professional UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a responsive dashboard component that displays AI-generated results with a clean, modern interface.

steps.title: Secure the Data Layer
steps.description: Implement authentication and database schemas to make your app functional for real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a secure Supabase schema for user profile management and data persistence within the Antigravity boilerplate.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Move your local project to a live URL to showcase your work to the world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for Vercel and generate a production build script.

pro_tips: Never hardcode environment variables; always use .env.example files to teach recruiters how to run your repo.
Write a high-quality README.md that explains the 'why' behind your tech stack choices, not just the 'how'.
Include a 'Demo Video' link in your README; recruiters rarely clone and run code, but they always watch a 60-second Loom.
Use semantic commit messages to demonstrate professional version control habits during your development process.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your project documentation to show you understand product scalability.
content.next_steps: After deploying your first project, refactor one specific component to improve performance or add a secondary feature. Publish a 'Build Log' post on LinkedIn or X tagging your stack, then move on to building a more complex, multi-agent system.
content.conversion_block: Your portfolio is the most important document in your career. Stop waiting for permission to be a developer and start defining your own path by shipping real-world software. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
